epee: noun. A sharp-pointed dueling sword with a bell-shaped guard, used (with the end blunted) in sport fencing.
err: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Erre.
errs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of err
ers: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ersu.
pee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Taje.
peer: verb. To look with difficulty, or as if searching for something.
per: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Persian.
perse: noun. A very dark (almost black) purple or blue-gray colour.
perverse: adjective. Turned aside while against something, splitting off from a thing.
pes: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iranian Persian.
pree: verb. to heed, to take notice of or pay attention to
prees: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of pree
preserve: noun. A sweet spread made of any of a variety of fruits.
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
reeve: noun. Any of several local officials, with varying responsibilities.
resee: verb. To see again or anew.
see: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seneca.
seep: verb. To ooze or pass slowly through pores or other small openings, and in overly small quantities; said of liquids, etc.
seer: noun. One who foretells the future; a clairvoyant, prophet, soothsayer or diviner.
ser: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Serrano.
serer: adjective. comparative form of sere: more sere
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veep: noun. Abbreviation of Vice President of the United States; the office of Vice President of the United States, especially during an election
veer: verb. To let out (a sail-line), to allow (a sheet) to run out.
vesper: noun. The bell that summons worshippers to vespers; the vesper-bell.
